records.ety: [OE. taluh, talugh; akin to OD. talgh, D. talk, G., Dan. and Sw. talg, Icel. tōlgr, tōlg, tōlk; and perhaps to Goth. tulgus firm.]

defs: The suet or fat of animals of the sheep and ox kinds, separated from membranous and fibrous matter by melting.
notes: ☞ The solid consistency of tallow is due to the large amount of stearin it contains. See Fat.

defs: The fat of some other animals, or the fat obtained from certain plants, or from other sources, resembling the fat of animals of the sheep and ox kinds.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Tallowed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Tallowing.]

defs: To grease or smear with tallow.
To cause to have a large quantity of tallow; to fatten; as, tallow sheep.
